data.title: Mach Natural Resources plans 9M unit sale; holders offload stake
data.description:
• On Monday, Mach Natural Resources (MNR) announced a secondary offering of ~9M common units as selling unitholders—VEPU, Simlog, and Sabinal Energy Operating—plan to offload their stakes.
  • The company will not issue any units or receive any proceeds from the offering.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to buy up to 1.35M additional units. Furthermore, the offering closing isn’t guaranteed.
  • MNR traded lower by 5.23% to $13.41 in after-hours on Apr. 6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Enphase Energy to sell $235M of advanced manufacturing production tax credits
data.description:
Enphase Energy (ENPH) disclosed post-market Monday it agreed to sell to an unnamed financial institution $235M of advanced manufacturing production tax credits generated by the production and sale of certain eligible components in the U.S. during 2025, pursuant to Section 45X of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.

Enphase (ENPH) said the tax credits were sold at 93% of face value, resulting in a ~$16.5M discount; because the amounts relate to tax credits generated in the prior fiscal year and do not reflect ongoing operating performance, the company expects to exclude them from its non-GAAP financial measures for Q1 2026.

By including the amounts in its GAAP financial measures, Enphase (ENPH) expects its GAAP gross margin for Q1 2026 to be reduced by ~6.7 percentage points compared to the company's prior guidance.

Enphase (ENPH) shares slipped 3.7% in regular trading Monday to $33.64, its lowest close YTD, extending the stock's weak performance during the past two months, as broader tariff concerns have weighed on clean energy names across the board.

data.title: Casey's General Stores set to join S&P 500
data.description:
• S&P MidCap 400 constituent Casey's General Stores (CASY) will replace Hologic (HOLX) in the S&P 500.
  • Additionally, S&P SmallCap 600 constituent DigitalOcean (DOCN) will replace Casey's General Stores in the S&P MidCap 400, and Broadstone Net Lease (BNL) will replace DigitalOcean in the S&P SmallCap 600, effective before the opening of trading on Thursday, April 9.
  • Affiliates of Blackstone and TPG Global are acquiring Hologic in a deal expected to be completed on or about April 7.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Betterware de México, S.A.P.I. de C.V. appoints Raúl del Villar as CFO
data.description:
• Betterware de México, S.A.P.I. de C.V., (BWMX) appointed Raúl del Villar as CFO, bringing 30+ years of senior finance experience to strengthen its financial leadership.
  • His expertise in multinational consumer companies like Grupo Axo (AXO) and Adidas (ADDYY) is expected to support the company’s growth strategy and long-term plans.
  • The BWMX stock closed at $17.47, up 3.25% in the regular session, and remained flat in after-hours trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold and silver little changed as Trump's Iran war deadline nears
data.description:
Gold and silver futures settled roughly flat Monday as investors awaited further news on the Middle East ahead of President Trump's stated deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Trump reasserted his Tuesday 8 p.m. deadline for Iran to reopen the vital oil shipping lane or face bombardment of its civilian infrastructure such as power plants and bridges, telling a news conference that "the entire country can be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night."

Earlier, Iran rejected a proposal from the U.S. and regional mediators to end hostilities for 45 days in exchange for opening the Strait of Hormuz.

Investor "focus is likely to remain on the war and interest rates. If the conflict drags on, oil will grind higher amid tightening supply conditions, adding to inflationary pressures," TD Securities global head of commodity strategy Bart Melek said in a note. "That leaves central ​banks, particularly the Federal Reserve, with less room to ease policy and could even revive discussions ‌about ⁠higher rates if energy prices rise further, which is negative for gold."

The Fed held rates last month, and a majority of traders now see no chance of the U.S. central bank ⁠cutting ​interest rates this year, according to CME's FedWatch ​tool.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed up 0.1% to $4,656.80/oz, its fifth gain in the past six sessions, and front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled down 0.1% to $72.661oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM) (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (DUST), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: White House's Hassett says AI, fiscal policy should allow Fed to resume rate cuts
data.description:
Kevin Hassett, director of the White House's National Economic Council, expects U.S. production to surge due to fiscal policy fueling factory construction and AI increasing productivity, all of which should lower inflation, he said on Monday.

He expects that inflationary pressures from the Iran war, which has led to soaring oil prices, should quickly abate once the conflict is resolved. Furthermore, the effect from war should be viewed as a one-off increase to prices, which the Federal Reserve typically “looks through” when setting monetary policy. Meanwhile, the infrastructure investment agreements secured by the Trump administration, combined with increased use of AI, are expected to kick in.

"$18T of new spending, AI increasing productivity — it puts downward pressure on inflation, and that should take the pressure off the Fed," he said in an interview on CNBC. "They should be able to lower rates, and I expect that that’s what’s going to happen when my friend Kevin Warsh gets in there."

The Senate plans to hold its confirmation hearings for Kevin Warsh as Fed chair on April 16, according to reporting by Politico. Warsh's confirmation, though, could be delayed by months as some senators object to the Justice Department's investigation into current Fed Chair Jerome Powell's handling of the Fed's multibillion-dollar renovation project. Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC), who is on the Senate Banking Committee, has said he'll block a vote on any candidate for the Fed chair as long as the DOJ probe of Powell continues.

Hassett calls the Iran war's impact on oil and inflation a "temporary disruption."  He focuses on the expected increase in construction, noting that the NEC is tracking 100 groundbreakings due to deals the administration has reached. That has increased employment in the construction industry in the last few months, he said. Eventually, that will lead to increased employment in the factories and higher production.

"Again, you can’t have a big increase in supply without having downward pressure on prices," Hassett said. "It’s very, very simple economics."

Traders, though, aren't expecting the Fed to lower rates anytime soon.  The probability that the central bank will keep its benchmark rate unchanged at 3.50%-3.75% at the Federal Open Market Committee's December meeting is 78.2%, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Geospace Technologies to cut 20% of workforce
data.description:
Geospace Technologies (GEOS) announced on Monday that executive management has implemented an organizational change plan that will result in a 20% reduction in its global workforce, included a voluntary early retirement plan available to eligible qualifying employees.

The company said management is evaluating opportunities to operate more efficiently by optimizing its cost structure.

In connection with the workforce reduction, the company expects to incur $0.6M in termination costs in the second quarter and $0.7M in costs in the third quarter.

According to the company’s SEC filing, the workforce reduction, along with cost-containment measures, is expected to produce about $10M in annual cash savings.

data.title: Exponent announces leadership changes effective May 2026
data.description:
• Exponent (EXPO) appointed John Pye as president and Eric Anderson as chief financial officer effective May 1, 2026.
  • Current CFO Richard Schlenker has been nominated to stand for election to the board of directors.
  • Karen Richardson will become chairman of the board following Paul Johnston’s retirement on June 4, 2026.
  • John Pye has been with Exponent since 1999 and has held multiple senior leadership roles across engineering, technology, and operations.
  • Pye currently serves as Vice President of Global Offices and Innovation.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Monday after market: SOC, OWL, MS
data.description:
U.S. stocks finished higher on Monday as traders remained focused on developments in the Middle East, including the status of the Strait of Hormuz.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) closed +0.5%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended +0.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished +0.4%.

Here are the 3 stocks to watch on Monday after market:

Sable Offshore (SOC) closed down 7.9% in Monday’s session after California Attorney General Rob Bonta formally announced the state’s opposition to the company’s request for a federal safety waiver to transport oil through Santa Barbara and Kern counties. Bonta argued that the U.S. Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration lacks jurisdiction to issue a special permit because the pipelines are intrastate and therefore subject to state regulation.

Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) edged up 0.3% in after-hours trading following a record low close of $8.44, as concerns over the $1.8T private credit market continue to pressure the stock. The company’s technology-focused OCIC fund received redemption requests exceeding 20% of its shares outstanding, far surpassing its 5% quarterly limit on share redemptions.

Morgan Stanley (MS) is in focus following an SEC filing revealing plans to launch the North Haven Strategic Credit Fund, a non-diversified, closed-end interval fund offering access to a broad spectrum of credit strategies. The fund will invest in private corporate loans, hybrid financing solutions, asset-based loans, syndicated loans, high-yield bonds, and emerging market debt, with quarterly repurchase offers of between 5% and 25% of outstanding shares at net asset value.

data.title: Panama set to authorize First Quantum to extract and ship from shuttered copper mine
data.description:
Panama's government said Monday it expects to issue a resolution authorizing First Quantum Minerals (FQVLF) to remove and process stockpiled ore at the company's shuttered Cobre Panama copper mine, marking a key step in managing the idled operation.

The document is in its final stage within the ministry and would enable First Quantum (FQVLF) to begin shipping the ore out of the country, Commerce and Industries Minister Julio Moltó said, according to Mining.com.

The stockpile is expected to yield ~70K metric tons of copper, with proceeds from concentrate sales potentially offsetting preservation costs in 2026, depending on regulatory timing.

The move advances Panama's broader effort to manage ~38M metric tons of ore mined before the operation was halted in 2023.

Cobre Panama produced 350K tons of copper in 2022; the government aims to decide on the project's long-term future by June.
